title: Choose a branching model to fit release cadence
description: Pick trunk-based or a release-branch model from how often the team ships, not by habit, and keep long-lived branches few.
body: Use when starting a repo or when branch sprawl and stale long-lived branches are slowing integration. Do not run a heavyweight gitflow with many long-lived branches on a team that ships continuously. Actions: inspect merge frequency and release cadence; pick trunk-based with short feature branches for frequent shipping, or a release-branch model only when supporting parallel released versions; document where hotfixes land. Validation signals include short-lived branches that merge within days and a main branch that stays releasable. Risk boundary: changing the model affects everyone, so agree it with the team before rewriting workflow docs.
